revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chausubaru Kofun Cluster]][[ja:茶臼原古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Chausubaru Kofun Cluster |native_name =茶臼原古墳群 |alternate_name = |image =Koyanezuka Kofun.jpg|alt = |caption = Koyanezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Chausubaru Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates ={{coord|32|08|36|N|131|24|51|E|region:JP-45|display=inline|qq=}}; {{Coord|32.138667|N|131.418694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|32.138675|N|131.418686|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| location ={{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|qid=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Saito|qid=Q850479|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis | part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = | built =c. 3rd to 5th century AD |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|08|36|N|131|24|51|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|Historic Site= {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Chausubaru Kofun Cluster'''|茶臼原古墳群| Chausubaru kofungun|qq=}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Chausubaru neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|qid=Q850479|lt=Saito|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} in [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217919|title= 茶臼原古墳群|trans-title= Chausubaru Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=November 1, 2023|qq=}}</ref> ==Background== The Chausubaru Kofun Cluster is located on a plateau at an elevation of about 130 meters on the northeast bank of the Hitotsuse River that flows southeast from Hitotsuse Dam through Saito City and into the {{ill|Hyūga Sea|en|Hyūga Sea|qid=Q7440098|lt=Hyūga Sea|1=Hyūga Sea}}. The cluster consists of a total of 55 confirmed Kofuns, including three {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and 52 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=}}-style round tombs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> Of the 55 ''kofun'', the one with the most [[archaeological excavation]] research is the {{nihongo|'''Koyanezuka Kofun'''|児屋根塚古墳|||qq=}}, which is 110 meters long with a 64-meter diameter circular portion and 61 meter wide rectangular portion and height of seven meters. It is orientated to the south and was surrounded by a shield-shaped moat. It is estimated to date from the early to mid-5th century. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included a {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} encased with clay and engraved with four mythical beasts and increased with 24 ''{{ill|Kanji|en|Kanji|qid=Q82772|lt=Kanji|1=Kanji}}'', a serpentine sword, iron straight sword and a large number of round, tubular and ''[[magatama]]'' beads. Per legend, the tumulus was the tomb of the ''[[kami]]'' [[Ame-no-Koyane]], after whom the tumulus was named. Some of the tumuli in the group are estimated to be older than the Koyanezuka Kofun, but most have not been investigated and the details are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|qid=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}} ==References== {{Reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1845 Miyazaki Prefecture Cultural Heritage]{{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
